jobs.name: AI-accelerated engineering tweet approval queue
jobs.prompt:
Use the ai-tech-twitter-system skill. Prepare Shivang's daily must-post X/Twitter approval queue around one core narrative: how Shivang uses AI to build/work dramatically faster and become a dramatically better engineer.

Positioning:
- The theme is "AI as engineering leverage": using AI agents, LLMs, automation, evals, code review, debugging, research, and tool-building to move faster while improving quality.
- It is okay to use strong language like "100x faster" or "100x better engineer" when framed as a builder ambition/operating philosophy, but avoid unverifiable claims that sound fake. Prefer: "feels like", "the path to", "compounds toward", "turns X into Y", or concrete examples of where AI compresses cycles.
- Make tweets sound like Shivang is actively building with AI, not merely commenting on AI news.

Source priority:
1. Inspect recent safe local work signals in /Users/shivang/dev/AIConcierge321 using git status/log/diff and lightweight file inspection. Look for examples of AI-assisted building, agent workflows, backend reliability, debugging velocity, product iteration, testing, observability, vendor integrations, infra automation, and architecture decisions.
2. Use session_search for recent Shivang discussions/work related to AI agents, LLM tooling, coding workflows, automation, debugging, learning, evals, product building, or becoming a stronger engineer with AI.
3. Use current AI/tech news only if it supports the "AI makes builders faster/better" narrative with a concrete mechanism.

Content requirements:
- Draft exactly 3 tweet candidates under 260 characters each.
- Every candidate must connect AI to building faster AND/OR becoming a better engineer: faster feedback loops, more ambitious scope, better debugging, sharper architecture, stronger tests/evals, better docs, safer refactors, deeper research, more production reliability.
- At least 2 of 3 drafts should be grounded in Shivang's own work/learning rather than external news.
- Include concrete engineering nouns where natural: agent workflows, tool calls, eval loops, traces, code review, test generation, debugging, refactors, Redis/Postgres, FastAPI, WebSockets, queues, retries, observability, deployment, vendor integrations.
- Avoid pure hype. The tweet should explain the mechanism behind the speed/quality jump.
- Preserve confidentiality: no secrets, customer data, private metrics, tokens, or exact sensitive vendor details.
- Tone: ambitious, concise, technical, builder/operator voice; useful to founders and US recruiters; no job-seeker framing or engagement bait.

Output format:
Daily AI-accelerated engineering tweet queue — <date>

1) <tweet candidate>
Why this works: <one sentence explaining the speed/quality/engineering signal>

2) <tweet candidate>
Why this works: <one sentence explaining the speed/quality/engineering signal>

3) <tweet candidate>
Why this works: <one sentence explaining the speed/quality/engineering signal>

Optional thread idea: <short outline for a thread on using AI to build faster/better, or 'None today'>

Approval: Reply with the number you approve, edits you want, or 'skip'. I will only post after explicit approval.

jobs.name: AI-accelerated engineering 45m tweet scan
jobs.prompt:
Use the ai-tech-twitter-system skill. Run a selective 45-minute opportunity scan for extra X/Twitter posts for Shivang around one core narrative: how Shivang uses AI to build/work dramatically faster and become a dramatically better engineer.

Operating mode:
- This is NOT a must-post queue. Only send a candidate when there is a genuinely strong AI-related angle worth interrupting for.
- Never post automatically. Ask for approval for any candidate.
- If no strong opportunity exists, return exactly: [SILENT]

Positioning:
- The theme is "AI as engineering leverage": AI agents, LLMs, automation, evals, code review, debugging, research, and tool-building compress feedback loops while improving engineering judgment.
- Strong phrases like "100x faster" or "100x better engineer" are allowed when framed as ambition/philosophy or backed by a mechanism. Avoid sounding like a fake metric.
- Make the candidate sound like Shivang is actively building with AI, not merely commenting on AI news.

Active-window guidance:
- Prefer useful opportunities for Europe/London + US attention windows. If the current local time is outside roughly 12:45 PM–3:45 AM IST and there is no urgent high-quality opportunity, return [SILENT].

Source priority:
1. Recent safe local work signals in /Users/shivang/dev/AIConcierge321: AI-assisted building, agent workflows, debugging velocity, backend reliability, evals, observability, payments/refunds/trust states, vendor integrations, Redis/Postgres/FastAPI/WebSockets, infra automation.
2. Recent session_search context about Shivang's AI building, coding workflows, debugging, learning, model/tooling experiments, or technical observations.
3. Current AI/tech news only if it maps to a concrete lesson about using AI to build faster or become a better engineer.

Candidate criteria:
- Must connect AI to building faster AND/OR becoming a better engineer: faster feedback loops, more ambitious scope, better debugging, sharper architecture, stronger tests/evals, safer refactors, deeper research, more production reliability.
- Prefer specific mechanisms over broad claims: agent routing, tool calls, traces, eval loops, code review, retries, queues, latency/cost budgets, auth gates, terminal states, HITL review, vendor fallback paths.
- Under 260 characters.
- No secrets, customer data, private metrics, tokens, or sensitive vendor details.
- Tone: ambitious, concise, technical, builder/operator voice useful to founders and US recruiters; no engagement bait.

If a strong opportunity exists, output:
AI engineering leverage opportunity — <date/time IST>

1) <tweet candidate>
Why now: <one sentence>
Why this works: <one sentence explaining the speed/quality/engineering signal>

Approval: Reply 'approve 1', edits, or 'skip'. I will only post after explicit approval.

If not strong, output only [SILENT].

jobs.name: Hourly sync personal Hermes setup to GitHub
jobs.prompt: Run the script `sync_personal_hermes_repo.py` to export Shivang's Hermes Agent config, skills, plugins, cron definitions, memories, scripts, and hooks into the public GitHub repository `shivang2000/personal-hermes-agent-setup`. The script must sanitize/redact secrets, skip auth tokens/logs/sessions/caches/binaries, commit only when changed, and push to GitHub. It should be quiet when there are no changes and print a short success message only when a sync commit is pushed. If the script detects possible secrets or fails, deliver the error.

origin.chat_name: Shivang Hermes AI / #general / i would like to save you on my github repo so can we do that ??

jobs.name: Tech / AI / YC / YouTube news digest for #tech-news
jobs.prompt:
You are posting into Shivang's Discord #tech-news channel/thread. Create a high-signal digest of new tech news, AI news/updates, notable repositories, YouTube tech/AI videos, Hacker News/YC items, YC company/product launches, and important strike/industry updates.

Timing/context: This job runs at 12:55, 16:55, 20:55, and 00:55 IST to cover European and US active hours without spamming. Prioritize things that became newly relevant since the previous run or within the last 24 hours.

Research sources to check each run, using available tools only:
- Hacker News front page / newest / Launch HN / Show HN / Ask HN when relevant.
- YC news/company/startup launches: Y Combinator site, YC Launch, Launch HN, and credible startup/product launch sources.
- AI labs and infra sources: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, xAI, Mistral, Hugging Face, Cursor/Windsurf/Cognition/Replit, model providers, eval/observability/inference infra companies.
- GitHub: trending/new notable AI/devtools/backend/infra repositories; include stars only if verified.
- YouTube: public tech/AI channel uploads and relevant videos. If account-specific YouTube subscriptions are not available, use public web/YouTube search and clearly avoid claiming it came from Shivang's subscriptions.
- X/Twitter: use accessible public web search only; do not fabricate tweets or metrics.
- Industry/strike updates: include only credible, relevant labor/service/platform strike updates likely to affect tech, AI, transport, delivery, media, or business operations.

Selection rules:
- Prefer concrete engineering/AI infrastructure/product news over generic hype.
- Avoid duplicates from the last run if visible in context; otherwise dedupe within this digest.
- Never invent links, stats, repo stars, authors, tweets, or release details. If unsure, say "not verified" or omit.
- Include only 8-15 items unless there is major breaking news.

Discord output format:
1. Title: "Tech / AI News Digest — <date/time IST>"
2. "Top picks" with 3-5 bullets, each: headline + link + why it matters in 1 sentence.
3. Sections as useful: AI/model updates, Devtools/repos, Hacker News/YC, YouTube, Strike/industry updates.
4. End with "Watchlist" for 2-4 developing stories to keep monitoring.

Tone: concise, useful, technical, no fluff. Make it feel curated for an engineer/founder tracking AI, infra, startups, and new tools.

origin.chat_name: Shivang Hermes AI / #tech-news / A Hr miss this new channel purpose of this channel is for you to create share...
